term.label: sample type
term.definition: Type of sample: enzyme_variant for enzyme samples, pet_substrate for PET substrate samples. Create one Sample row for every distinct enzyme variant and one Sample row for every distinct PET substrate material reported in the paper or supporting information; never collapse multiple variants or substrates into one row.

term.label: enzyme identifier
term.definition: Unique identifier for one enzyme variant Sample row. Every wild type and every distinct mutation combination must receive its own row and identifier, including comparator enzymes. Used to link Sample rows to Assay rows. Populated when sample type is enzyme_variant.

term.label: substrate identifier
term.definition: Unique identifier for one PET substrate Sample row. Every distinct material, supplier, physical form, or crystallinity condition must receive its own row and identifier. Used to link Sample rows to Assay rows. Populated when sample type is pet_substrate.

term.label: enzyme type
term.definition: Name or identifier of the PET hydrolase enzyme variant used in the experiment. Includes wild-type enzymes (IsPETase, LCC, TfCut2, DuraPETase, etc.) and engineered variants with specific mutations. Populated when sample type is enzyme_variant.

term.label: enzyme mutation
term.definition: Specific amino acid substitutions in the enzyme variant, using standard notation (e.g., F243I for Phe→Ile at position 243). Multiple mutations separated by '/'. Populated when sample type is enzyme_variant.

term.label: enzyme organism source
term.definition: Source organism or environmental library from which the enzyme was originally isolated. For metagenomic enzymes (e.g. LCC from leaf-branch compost metagenome), specify the library origin. Populated when sample type is enzyme_variant.

term.label: enzyme melting temperature
term.definition: Protein melting temperature (Tm) measured by differential scanning fluorimetry (DSF) or circular dichroism (CD). The temperature at which 50% of the protein is unfolded. Populated when sample type is enzyme_variant.

term.label: PET substrate type
term.definition: Type and commercial origin of the PET substrate material used in the depolymerisation assay. Includes amorphous/crystalline state, form factor, and supplier information. Populated when sample type is pet_substrate.

term.label: substrate form factor
term.definition: Physical form of the PET substrate. Controlled vocabulary: powder | film | flake | nanoparticle | microplastic | fibre | pellet | preform | textile | other. Populated when sample type is pet_substrate.

term.label: PET crystallinity
term.definition: Percentage crystallinity of the PET substrate as measured by differential scanning calorimetry (DSC). Lower crystallinity generally correlates with higher enzymatic degradation rates. Populated when sample type is pet_substrate.

term.label: PET glass transition temperature
term.definition: Glass transition temperature (Tg) of the PET substrate. PET enzymatic degradation rate increases significantly above Tg due to increased polymer chain mobility. Populated when sample type is pet_substrate.

term.label: PET molecular weight
term.definition: Number-average (Mn) or weight-average (Mw) molecular weight of the PET substrate. Populated when sample type is pet_substrate.

term.label: assay identifier
term.definition: Unique identifier for one enzymatic depolymerisation assay condition. Create separate Assay rows for distinct enzyme-substrate-condition combinations; do not encode multiple variants or substrates in one assay row.

term.label: assay name
term.definition: Descriptive name of the assay, typically encoding the substrate, scale, and purpose.

term.label: enzyme sample identifier
term.definition: Reference to the enzyme variant Sample row used in this assay. Must match an enzyme identifier in the Sample sheet.

term.label: substrate sample identifier
term.definition: Reference to the PET substrate Sample row used in this assay. Must match a substrate identifier in the Sample sheet.

term.label: buffer type
term.definition: Buffer system used in the reaction. Controlled vocabulary: potassium phosphate | sodium phosphate | glycine-NaOH | Tris-HCl | HEPES | borate | water (pH-stat) | other.

term.label: buffer concentration
term.definition: Molar concentration of the reaction buffer.

term.label: reaction pH
term.definition: pH at which the enzymatic depolymerisation reaction was conducted. Most PET hydrolases have optimal activity at pH 7-9.

term.label: reaction temperature
term.definition: Temperature at which the enzymatic depolymerisation reaction was conducted. Optimal temperatures for thermostable PET hydrolases range from 50-72 °C.

term.label: enzyme loading
term.definition: Amount of enzyme used relative to substrate mass. Common units: mg enzyme/g PET, µM, or mg/mL.

term.label: substrate solid loading
term.definition: Concentration of PET substrate in the reaction mixture. Reported as g/L or as weight percentage (w/w).

term.label: reactor volume
term.definition: Total reaction volume of the depolymerisation assay.

term.label: agitation rate
term.definition: Shaking or stirring speed during the reaction.

term.label: reaction time
term.definition: Duration of the enzymatic depolymerisation reaction.

term.label: actual PET vs proxy substrate
term.definition: Whether this assay used actual PET plastic as substrate (yes) or a proxy substrate such as BHET, MHET, or pNPB (no). Controlled: yes | no | both.

term.label: analytical separation method
term.definition: Chromatographic or other separation method used for product analysis. Include column type if applicable. Replaces the former binary 'HPLC used' field to properly distinguish separation from detection.

term.label: detection method
term.definition: Detector type used to quantify depolymerisation products. Controlled: absorbance | fluorescence | refractive_index | mass_spectrometry | NaOH_titration | pH_stat | other.

term.label: UV detection wavelength
term.definition: UV wavelength used for absorbance-based detection of PET monomers. TPA is typically detected at 240-242 nm.

term.label: observation unit identifier
term.definition: Unique identifier for one observation/measurement row. Create separate rows for distinct assay, analyte, time point, or measurement type combinations.

term.label: assay identifier reference
term.definition: Reference to the Assay row this observation was measured from. Must match an assay identifier in the Assay sheet.

term.label: analyte identity
term.example: TPA | MHET | BHET | EG | total aromatic equivalents
term.definition: Identity of the analyte(s) quantified. Critical for interpreting depolymerisation percentage and product concentration values.

term.label: specific activity
term.definition: Enzyme specific activity: rate of PET depolymerisation per unit enzyme mass. Value and unit reported together. When comparing across studies, unit normalisation is required.

term.label: PET depolymerisation percentage
term.definition: Percentage of PET substrate converted to soluble monomers (TPA, MHET, BHET, EG) during the reaction.

term.label: product concentration
term.definition: Concentration of depolymerisation products (typically TPA equivalents) achieved in the reaction.

term.label: initial reaction rate
term.definition: Initial rate of product formation measured in the linear phase of the reaction, before significant substrate depletion or enzyme inactivation. Not the same as space-time yield or maximum rate. Must specify the time window and product used for calculation.

term.label: space-time yield
term.definition: Volumetric productivity of the reaction, expressed as mass of product per unit volume per unit time. Not the same as initial rate or specific activity.

term.label: catalytic rate constant kcat
term.definition: Catalytic rate constant (turnover number). Rarely reported for heterogeneous PET depolymerisation due to difficulty in defining effective enzyme concentration at the solid-liquid interface. Populated in 1/19 analysed papers. Must be interpreted alongside kinetic substrate basis and kinetic model.

term.label: Michaelis constant Km
term.definition: Michaelis-Menten constant. Challenging to measure for heterogeneous PET substrates; reported for soluble proxy substrates (BHET, pNPB). Populated in 1/19 analysed papers. Must be interpreted alongside kinetic substrate basis and kinetic model.

term.label: kinetic substrate basis
term.example: pNPB (soluble proxy) | BHET (soluble proxy) | amorphous PET film
term.definition: Substrate used for kinetic parameter determination. Critical for comparability: Km/kcat measured on soluble proxy substrates (pNPB, BHET, MHET) are not directly comparable to values from heterogeneous PET degradation.

term.label: kinetic model
term.definition: Kinetic model used to derive the reported parameters (e.g. Michaelis-Menten, substrate inhibition, interfacial kinetics).

term.label: half inactivation temperature
term.definition: Temperature at which the enzyme loses 50% of its initial activity after a defined incubation period. Populated in 0/19 analysed papers.

term.label: measurement type
term.definition: Whether depolymerisation was measured at a single endpoint or as a time-resolved series. Controlled: endpoint | time_resolved | both.

term.label: time point
term.definition: Time point at which the measurement was taken, relative to reaction start. Required for time-resolved measurements to enable temporal comparison.

term.label: replicate count
term.definition: Number of independent replicates for this measurement.

term.label: measurement uncertainty
term.definition: Uncertainty or error of the measurement, with indication of type (SD, SEM, 95% CI) and sample size.

term.label: measurement notes
term.example: Activity calculated from NaOH consumption between 10-90% conversion
term.definition: Additional notes about the measurement methodology, calculation approach, or data quality.

term.label: investigation contentUrl
term.definition: URL or DOI link to the investigation source document (e.g. the published paper). Populated from the paper DOI as https://doi.org/{doi}.

term.label: study identifier
term.definition: Identifier of the study this assay belongs to. Typically the DOI or internal study ID. Used to link Assay rows back to their parent Study row.
